prompt: The applyColorAdjustments function in src/js/utils/image-effects.ts is really slow on my Chromebook — it does pixel-by-pixel brightness, contrast, HSL hue/saturation shifts, gamma correction, sepia, and temperature adjustments in a JS for-loop. Can you convert this to a Rust WASM module? I want it to follow the same pattern as PyMuPDF loading and integrate with the existing WasmProvider.

assertions.text: Creates a Rust crate with Cargo.toml using wasm-bindgen and serde dependencies
Rust lib.rs implements all 8 color operations: brightness, contrast, saturation, hue shift, temperature, tint, gamma, sepia
Creates a TypeScript loader following cached-promise deduplication pattern (cachedModule + loadPromise)
Updates WasmProvider with new package type and CDN default
Preserves JS fallback — original image-effects.ts functions remain callable
Includes Rust unit tests for numerical correctness
